perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE II AGREEMENT TO COOPERATE Subject to the limitations set forth in Article III, Seller and CompuCom agree that with respect to each account listed on Exhibit A hereto, --------- that until the earlier of (a) May 11, 2000 or (b) the termination of the existing contract between Seller and such account (i) each party will cooperate with the other in delivering Services and Products to such account in substantially the same manner in which such Services and Products were delivered to such -2- <PAGE> account prior to Closing and (ii) each party's representative will be permitted to call on such account.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE II AGREEMENT TO COOPERATE Subject to the limitations set forth in Article III, Seller and CompuCom agree that with respect to each account listed on Exhibit A hereto, --------- that until the earlier of (a) May 11, 2000 or (b) the termination of the existing contract between Seller and such account (i) each party will not cooperate with the other in delivering Services and Products to such account in substantially the same manner in which such Services and Products were delivered to such -2- <PAGE> account prior to Closing and (ii) each party's representative will be permitted to call on such account.
perturbation.explanation: The original Article II explicitly states that 'each party will cooperate with the other'. Changing this to 'each party will not cooperate with the other' creates a direct, in-text contradiction. This contradicts the fundamental premise of Article II, which is centered around cooperation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE II AGREEMENT TO COOPERATE Subject to the limitations set forth in Article III, Seller and CompuCom agree that with respect to each account listed on Exhibit A hereto, --------- that until the earlier of (a) May 11, 2000 or (b) the termination of the existing contract between Seller and such account (i) each party will not cooperate with the other in delivering Services and Products to such account in substantially the same manner in which such Services and Products were delivered to such -2- <PAGE> account prior to Closing and (ii) each party's representative will be permitted to call on such account.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'each party will cooperate' to 'each party will not cooperate.' This directly contradicts the original intent and wording of Article II, which focuses on cooperation between Seller and CompuCom. Since the changed text and contradicted text are the same in this case, the change results in a clear in-text contradiction, as per the provided explanation and the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type definition.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Seller's Agreement. (a) Seller agrees that during the Non- ------------------ Competition Period Seller will not, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (i) engage in the Product Business; (ii) encourage any Service Account to specify a party other than CompuCom to provide Products and image load/configurations to such Service Account; or (iii) solicit, entice or induce any employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom to terminate his or her employment with CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Seller's Agreement. (a) Seller agrees that during the Non- ------------------ Competition Period Seller will not, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (i) engage in the Product Business; (ii) encourage any Service Account to specify a party other than CompuCom to provide Products and image load/configurations to such Service Account; or (iii) solicit, entice or induce any employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom to terminate his or her employment with CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom. Seller can however engage in the Product Business during the non-competition period if CompuCom is unable to fulfill the clients needs.
perturbation.explanation: Section 3.1(a) explicitly restricts Seller from engaging in the Product Business during the Non-Competition Period. Adding the sentence 'Seller can however engage in the Product Business during the non-competition period if CompuCom is unable to fulfill the clients needs' directly contradicts this restriction. This creates uncertainty regarding the extent of the non-compete agreement. This creates a clear in-text contradiction within Section 3.1(a) itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Seller's Agreement. (a) Seller agrees that during the Non- ------------------ Competition Period Seller will not, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (i) engage in the Product Business; (ii) encourage any Service Account to specify a party other than CompuCom to provide Products and image load/configurations to such Service Account; or (iii) solicit, entice or induce any employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom to terminate his or her employment with CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom. Seller can however engage in the Product Business during the non-competition period if CompuCom is unable to fulfill the clients needs.
perturbation.justification: The original text of section 3.1(a) strictly prohibits the Seller from engaging in the Product Business during the Non-Competition Period. The changed text adds a sentence that allows the Seller to engage in the Product Business during the Non-Competition Period if CompuCom is unable to fulfill the client's needs. This directly contradicts the initial prohibition, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text as it highlights where the contradiction is now present.

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE V NON-DISCLOSURE 5.1 Seller's Non-Disclosure Obligations. Seller covenants and agrees ----------------------------------- that, unless otherwise required by law, from and after the Closing: (a) Seller shall cooperate with CompuCom at CompuCom's expense to protect and safeguard all of CompuCom's Confidential Information; and (b) Seller shall not, directly or indirectly, use, sell, license, publish, disclose, or otherwise transfer or make available to others any of CompuCom's Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE V NON-DISCLOSURE 5.1 Seller's Non-Disclosure Obligations. Seller covenants and agrees ----------------------------------- that, unless otherwise required by law, from and after the Closing: (a) Seller shall not cooperate with CompuCom at CompuCom's expense to protect and safeguard all of CompuCom's Confidential Information; and (b) Seller shall not, directly or indirectly, use, sell, license, publish, disclose, or otherwise transfer or make available to others any of CompuCom's Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Section 5.1(a) originally states that the 'Seller shall cooperate with CompuCom to protect CompuCom's Confidential Information.' By changing this to 'Seller shall not cooperate with CompuCom', a direct in-text contradiction is introduced. It is now unclear to what extent or if the seller has the obligation to assist CompuCom regarding confidential information. This contradicts the overall purpose of the non-disclosure agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE V NON-DISCLOSURE 5.1 Seller's Non-Disclosure Obligations. Seller covenants and agrees ----------------------------------- that, unless otherwise required by law, from and after the Closing: (a) Seller shall not cooperate with CompuCom at CompuCom's expense to protect and safeguard all of CompuCom's Confidential Information; and (b) Seller shall not, directly or indirectly, use, sell, license, publish, disclose, or otherwise transfer or make available to others any of CompuCom's Confidential Information. As used in this Agreement, the terms "CompuCom's Confidential Information" means proprietary or confidential information concerning the Business including, without limitation, information regarding prices charged for Products, the assets, liabilities, and financial condition of the Business, names and identities of customers and analyses of the amount and types of Products purchased by each such customer. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller shall be permitted to disclose historical financial information, including financial information relating to the Business, as may be required by customers, vendors, lenders or other third parties, provided that such third parties shall agree to preserve the -------- confidentiality of such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 5.1(a) states that 'Seller shall cooperate with CompuCom', which implies assistance in protecting confidential information. The changed text modifies this to 'Seller shall not cooperate with CompuCom', creating a direct contradiction within Article V. This directly contradicts the initial intention of the agreement which is to protect CompuCom's Confidential Information, hence 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: 6.3 Successors and Assigns. This Agreement shall be binding upon and ---------------------- shall inure to the benefit of and be enforceable by the parties and their respective successors and assigns, including without limitation by merger or otherwise. This Agreement shall not be assignable.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.3 Successors and Assigns. This Agreement shall be binding upon and ---------------------- shall inure to the benefit of and be enforceable by the parties and their respective successors and assigns, including without limitation by merger or otherwise. This Agreement shall be assignable.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 6.3 explicitly states 'This Agreement shall not be assignable'. By changing this to 'This Agreement shall be assignable', a direct and irreconcilable in-text contradiction is created. This undermines the entire section on successors and assigns and it is unclear what the parties' intention is regarding the assignability of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.3 Successors and Assigns. This Agreement shall be binding upon and ---------------------- shall inure to the benefit of and be enforceable by the parties and their respective successors and assigns, including without limitation by merger or otherwise. This Agreement shall be assignable.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 6.3 explicitly prohibits assignment of the agreement ('This Agreement shall not be assignable'). The changed text directly contradicts this by stating ('This Agreement shall be assignable'). Since the contradicted text is the exact same changed text, the contradiction is self-evident and direct within the same section, satisfying the conditions for a strong in-text contradiction under 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction', where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications or by altering essential language as defined by 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.
